title: overlay.go ReadInput: defer unlock + panic recovery
description:
File: internal/session/client/overlay.go, lines 113-130

ReadInput() runs as a bare goroutine (go c.ReadInput(), line 238 in SetupInteractiveTerminal). It has a for loop that reads keyboard input and dispatches to mode handlers (HandlePassthroughBytes, HandleMenuBytes, HandleScrollBytes, HandleDefaultBytes). The VT mutex is locked/unlocked manually each iteration.

Current risk: If any handler panics, the goroutine dies silently, the mutex stays locked (deadlocking the entire session), and the user can no longer type input. There is no recover and the goroutine is never relaunched.

Fix:
1. Convert manual vt.Mu.Unlock() to defer-based unlock (wrap lock/unlock body in an anonymous func with defer).
2. Add a recover() wrapping the for loop body so that a panic in any handler doesn't kill the goroutine — it should log the panic and continue reading input on the next iteration.
